test_plan.strategy: Unit tests pin the two changed projection behaviors in snapshot-reader.js directly (projectFromSnapshot / new projectQualitySurfaces helper) with the full absent-tolerant matrix: key present with data, key present null, key absent. Integration tests exercise collectOne with a stubbed aitri binary emitting rc.161-shaped payloads to prove the .aitri.local read is (a) skipped when the payload carries the lastSession key — asserted by seeding a POISON .aitri.local whose value must NOT surface — and (b) still taken on key-absent payloads (pre-rc.161 CLI), pinning NFR-056. NFR-057/058 map onto the EXISTING aitri-reader / parseErrors / integration-alert suites run with unmodified assertions rather than duplicating them. FR-063 is asserted via evaluateIntegrationAlert against a manifest fixture with reviewedUpTo 2.0.0-rc.161 (the live ~/.aitri-hub edit happens at ship, outside tests). All fixtures use os.tmpdir()/AITRI_HUB_DIR isolation; no absolute user paths.
test_plan.coverage_goal: every MUST FR and every Regression NFR traced to at least one passing automated test; the new helper at 100% branch coverage of its decision points

test_cases.title: lastSession from snapshot wins; .aitri.local never read
preconditions: stub aitri binary emits payload with lastSession {at, agent, event}
project dir contains a POISON .aitri.local with a DIFFERENT lastSession
test_cases.given: a snapshot-eligible local project whose stubbed status --json carries lastSession {event:'approve 1', agent:'claude', at:'2026-07-07T10:00:00.000Z'} and a .aitri.local on disk with lastSession {event:'POISON'}
test_cases.then: the record's lastSession.event is 'approve 1' (snapshot value) and the POISON value appears nowhere in the record
test_cases.expected_result: snapshot lastSession carried verbatim; poison never surfaces, proving no .aitri.local read on this path

test_cases.title: lastSession key present but null: trusted as null, no fallback
test_cases.given: a stubbed status --json whose payload contains the lastSession KEY with value null, and a poison .aitri.local with a real session object
test_cases.then: record.lastSession is null and the poison .aitri.local value does not surface
steps: seed payload with lastSession:null + poison file
test_cases.expected_result: key-presence proves the rc.161 contract; null means 'no session yet', not 'fall back'

test_cases.title: payload without lastSession key: legacy fallback still populates
preconditions: stub payload LACKS the lastSession key (pre-rc.161 shape)
test_cases.given: a stubbed pre-rc.161 status --json payload (no lastSession key) and a .aitri.local with lastSession {event:'checkpoint'}
test_cases.then: record.lastSession.event is 'checkpoint' — the inline fallback ran exactly as before
test_cases.expected_result: pre-rc.161 projects keep their lastSession via the fallback (NFR-056 half)

test_cases.title: quality_gates projected verbatim into qualitySurfaces
test_cases.given: a snapshot object whose tests.perPipeline root entry has quality_gates [{name:'lint',status:'fail',required:true}]
test_cases.then: record.qualitySurfaces.perPipeline[0] has scope 'root' and quality_gates [{name:'lint',status:'fail',required:true}]
test_cases.expected_result: gate name, status and required preserved verbatim

test_cases.title: coverage-style gate keeps threshold and measured
test_cases.given: a root perPipeline entry with quality_gates [{name:'coverage',status:'pass',required:true,threshold:80,measured:92}]
test_cases.then: the projected gate carries threshold 80 and measured 92 as numbers
test_cases.expected_result: optional numeric fields carried when present

test_cases.title: all-null quality surfaces: record carries no qualitySurfaces key
test_cases.given: a snapshot whose every tests.perPipeline entry has quality_gates: null and ac_coverage: null (and a second snapshot lacking tests.perPipeline entirely)
test_cases.then: 'qualitySurfaces' in record === false for both — no key, not an empty object
test_cases.expected_result: absent-tolerant: old CLIs and gate-less projects produce records without the new key (NFR-056 half)

test_cases.title: ac_coverage passes through unchanged
test_cases.given: a root perPipeline entry with ac_coverage [{ac_id:'AC-001',fr_id:'FR-001',tests_passing:2,tests_failing:0,tests_skipped:0,tests_manual:0,status:'covered'}]
test_cases.then: the projected entry's ac_coverage deep-equals the input array (identical, no re-shaping)
test_cases.expected_result: pass-through unchanged per FR-062

test_cases.title: ac_coverage null on a pipeline that has gates: entry carries gates only
test_cases.given: a root entry with quality_gates non-null and ac_coverage: null
test_cases.then: the projected perPipeline entry has a quality_gates key and NO ac_coverage key
test_cases.expected_result: each surface is independently absent-tolerant

test_cases.title: integration alert silent for rc.161 once reviewedUpTo advances
test_cases.given: evaluateIntegrationAlert with detected aitriVersion '2.0.0-rc.161' and a manifest whose reviewedUpTo is '2.0.0-rc.161'
test_cases.then: no version-gap integration alert is raised
test_cases.expected_result: reviewed baseline covers rc.161

test_cases.title: new projection cases run with zero skips
test_cases.given: the snapshot-reader test file containing the TC-060/061/062 cases
test_cases.when: npm test executes the unit suite
test_cases.then: all new projection cases run and pass; none is marked skip/todo
steps: assert zero skipped among the new cases
test_cases.expected_result: FR-047's skipped-placeholder pattern is not repeated

test_cases.title: ac_coverage empty array passes through as empty array, not dropped
test_cases.given: a root perPipeline entry with ac_coverage: [] (present but empty — distinct from null)
test_cases.then: the projected entry carries ac_coverage: [] — present-but-empty is preserved, never coerced to key-absence
test_cases.expected_result: pass-through fidelity includes the empty array (null and [] stay distinguishable end-to-end)

test_cases.title: version beyond the reviewed baseline still alerts
test_cases.given: a manifest fixture with reviewedUpTo '2.0.0-rc.161' and a project detected on aitriVersion '2.0.0-rc.162'
test_cases.then: the version-gap alert IS raised for rc.162 — advancing the baseline to rc.161 silences only reviewed versions
test_cases.expected_result: the baseline advance does not blind the Hub to future contract changes

test_cases.title: malformed manifest leaves alert evaluation on its existing degraded path
test_cases.given: an unreadable/invalid integration-compat.json fixture and a project on '2.0.0-rc.161'
test_cases.then: behavior matches the pre-existing malformed-manifest contract (no crash; same alert outcome as before this feature) — pinned by running the existing manifest test suite unmodified
steps: assert no throw and unchanged outcome
test_cases.expected_result: FR-063 is a data edit; the reader's failure modes are untouched

test_cases.title: new projection test file is discovered by the npm test glob
test_cases.given: the new test file placed under tests/unit/ with the *.test.js suffix
test_cases.when: npm test resolves its file list
test_cases.then: the new file is in the executed set (its tests appear in the runner output)
steps: assert the new cases' titles appear in output
test_cases.expected_result: the cases cannot silently drop out of CI by living outside the glob

test_cases.title: this feature's build report carries no blocked-on-Core debt for the consumed fields
test_cases.given: features/contract-catchup-rc161/spec/04_BUILD_REPORT.json after Phase 4
test_cases.then: no entry references quality_gates/ac_coverage/lastSession as blocked-on-Core — the FR-047 debt closes here instead of moving forward
test_cases.expected_result: debt is closed with evidence, not re-filed

test_cases.title: pre-rc.161 payload produces a byte-identical record (regression pin)
test_cases.given: a stubbed pre-rc.161 status --json payload (no lastSession key, no quality_gates/ac_coverage on perPipeline) and a .aitri.local with a session
test_cases.when: collectOne collects the project before and after this feature's change (golden expectation from the pre-change projection)
test_cases.then: the produced record deep-equals the golden pre-change record: lastSession via fallback, and no qualitySurfaces key
test_cases.expected_result: old-CLI projects are provably untouched — the additive-only guardrail is executable, not asserted

test_cases.title: legacy fallback reader path untouched (existing suite runs unmodified)
test_cases.given: the existing aitri-reader test suite as committed on the parent branch, with zero assertion changes
test_cases.when: npm test runs after this feature's implementation
test_cases.then: every aitri-reader test passes without modification — the snapshot-ineligible path never entered this feature's diff
steps: assert aitri-reader suite green with no diff to its file
test_cases.expected_result: NFR-057 is enforced by the unmodified existing suite, per the test plan's mapping rule

test_cases.title: parseErrors consumption and version-gap alert untouched (existing suites run unmodified)
test_cases.given: the existing bugs.parseErrors and evaluateIntegrationAlert test suites as committed, with zero assertion changes
test_cases.when: npm test runs after this feature's implementation
test_cases.then: every parseErrors and integration-alert test passes without modification
steps: assert both suites green with no diffs to their files
test_cases.expected_result: rc.158 consumption and the alert engine are provably unaffected

test_cases.title: pre-rc.161 payload with no .aitri.local at all: record shape still identical
test_cases.given: a stubbed pre-rc.161 payload (no new keys) and NO .aitri.local on disk
test_cases.then: record.lastSession is null (fallback finds nothing, as today) and no qualitySurfaces key exists
test_cases.expected_result: the absent-file corner of the legacy path is inside the byte-identical guarantee

test_cases.title: golden-record comparison is not vacuous (mutation guard)
test_cases.given: the TC-056h golden record with one field deliberately mutated
test_cases.when: the same deep-equal assertion runs against the mutated copy
test_cases.then: the comparison FAILS — proving TC-056h's pass is a real byte-parity check, not a tautology
test_cases.expected_result: regression pin proven non-vacuous (rc159 NFR-041 precedent)

test_cases.title: snapshot-ineligible project: new projection never runs on the legacy path
test_cases.given: a project whose aitriVersion is below the snapshot floor (fallback reader path)
test_cases.then: the record carries no qualitySurfaces key and its lastSession comes from the legacy reader exactly as before
test_cases.expected_result: the feature's code is unreachable from aitri-reader.js

test_cases.title: corrupted .aitri on the legacy path degrades exactly as pre-feature
test_cases.given: a snapshot-ineligible project with a malformed .aitri
test_cases.then: the existing degraded-record behavior is produced, unchanged by this feature
test_cases.expected_result: legacy failure modes untouched

test_cases.title: bugs.parseErrors consumption coexists with the new rc.161 keys
test_cases.given: a stubbed rc.161 payload carrying BOTH quality surfaces AND bugs.parseErrors ['root']
test_cases.then: the record carries the parseErrors-driven flags exactly as today AND the new qualitySurfaces key — neither interferes with the other
test_cases.expected_result: rc.158 consumption is orthogonal to rc.161 consumption

test_cases.title: version-gap alert still fires for an unreviewed version carrying the new fields
test_cases.given: a manifest with reviewedUpTo '2.0.0-rc.161' and a project detected on an unreviewed '2.0.0-rc.199' whose payload includes qualitySurfaces data
test_cases.then: the alert fires — presence of the new fields never suppresses the version-gap check
test_cases.expected_result: alert logic reads versions, not payload keys
